title: LPR Expansion Plans, Data Retention Policy, and Community Concerns on ICE-Connected Vendors
summary: Continued discussion on Axon LPR contract. Council questions data breach liability (deferred to executive session). SPD plans to expand from 13 to 26 cameras at original proposed locations across all city quadrants, focusing on traffic convergence points. Community concern addressed about contracting with companies that also serve ICE — Chief argues restricting vendors by federal client list would severely limit SPD capability. Data retention: 30-day automatic deletion for unused bulk data; evidence extracted for investigations follows crime-specific retention per Criminal Procedure Law. Council member asks about cross-referencing LPR locations with Vision Zero data (no current correlation). Real-life example cited: north side homicide solved using LPR cameras. Meeting moves to executive session for contract terms.

speakers.name: Donna Moore

political_signal.description: Council pressing on data breach liability, retention policies, geographic equity of camera placement, and ethical concerns about vendor relationships with federal immigration enforcement
